title: No validation that required callbacks are set before calling them
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/app_interface/app_interface.c

The sink registration allows partial sinks with only some callbacks set. valid_sink() (lines 45-52) requires at least ONE callback to be non-NULL:

  static bool valid_sink(const struct lichen_app_interface_sink *sink)
  {
      return sink != NULL &&
             (sink->emit_text != NULL || sink->emit_status != NULL ||
              sink->submit_text != NULL ||
              sink->get_status != NULL || sink->get_config != NULL ||
              sink->set_config != NULL);
  }

The snapshot functions correctly filter by callback type (e.g., snapshot_text_sinks only includes sinks with emit_text != NULL).

However, provider_conflict() (lines 61-72) allows multiple sinks with the same callback type IF they are NULL:

  return (candidate->get_status != NULL && existing->get_status != NULL) ||
         (candidate_config && existing_config) ||
         (candidate->submit_text != NULL && existing->submit_text != NULL);

This allows scenarios where:
1. Sink A registers with emit_text=NULL, get_status=funcA
2. Sink B registers with emit_text=NULL, get_status=funcB
3. get_status conflict is correctly rejected

But actually wait, this is correct - it DOES reject the conflict. Looking more closely, the logic is sound.

However, a potential issue exists: if the callback signatures in lichen_app_interface_sink are changed, the filtering in snapshot functions must be updated in sync. This is a maintainability concern, not a runtime bug.

Closing this as false positive - the current code is correct.
